method_note: All entries surfaced via WebSearch this mission; verification level logged per entry. ctkjsps.com direct fetch failed (expired TLS cert); claims taken from search-level extracts of official pages.

sources.title: Shore Power - PowerCon A/S
sources.finding: Containerized 20-ft 'building block' shore-power systems, HV and LV, 'from the largest cruise ships to container ships to smaller fishing vessels and ferries' - the exact compact/modular tier V05 claims is empty.

sources.title: Port of Skagen Awards Shore Power Contract to PowerCon
sources.finding: 16 MW cruise shore-power facility awarded to PowerCon, completion end-2027, operations 2028 season - AFIR-deadline procurement already being let to incumbents pre-2029.

sources.title: iC7-Hybrid grid converter
sources.finding: Catalog grid-converter modules for shore supply; 100+ iC7-Hybrid units installed in Stockholm, Aalesund, Haugesund harbors per Danfoss marine pages.

sources.title: PSW & Danfoss: flexible OPS for cruise ships; FAYARD portable shore power
sources.finding: Danfoss+PSW OPS deployments; FAYARD case = portable shore power in two 20-ft containers (500 A @ 440 V / 300 A @ 690 V) - compact vessel-voltage-class products already shipping.

sources.title: Shore to Ship Shore Power Converter
sources.finding: Plug-and-play containerized IP65 shore power, scalable to 8 MW, 50/60 Hz, LV and MV distribution - catalog-style compact product.

sources.title: Marine Static Frequency Converter for Shore-to-Ship Power
sources.finding: Marine SFC product line for shore-to-ship, sub-MW to MW class.

sources.title: Marine Shore Power Solutions (EAS/EAC)
sources.finding: Containerized modular frequency-converter power blocks, 50/60/400 Hz, sized for cruise/Ro-Pax/container berths.

sources.title: Shore Power Frequency Converters
sources.finding: US vendor of shore-power frequency converters (Navy/port heritage) - occupies the US compact tier.

sources.title: INGEDRIVE Static Frequency Drives for OPS
sources.finding: Merchant SFC drives for onshore power supply.

sources.title: Shore-to-ship converters (ACS6080-SFC line)
sources.finding: Pre-fabricated, pre-tested containerized SFC modules, 'minimum time on site' - incumbents already sell the fast-install claim.

sources.title: Ship-side ShorePower / PowerFit
sources.finding: PowerFit: complete vessel-mounted 40-ft containerized HVSC package (reel + switchboard + transformer + controls); USD 5M multi-unit order incl. Evergreen/Seaspan/Capital (per safety4sea/container-news coverage) - vessel-side compact retrofit already productized.

sources.title: NIDEC ASI electric power solutions for ship-to-shore connection
sources.finding: Full shore-connection scope: MV switchboards, transformers, static converters, controls.

sources.title: 青岛创统科技 - 岸电电源/船舶岸电系统/变频电源
sources.finding: Founded 1990; lead drafter of national standard GB/T 25316 (static shore-power devices); CCS-certified; ~300 shore-power units in service at major Chinese ports; 85 patents. Chinese compact-converter incumbent that wrote the standard.
sources.verified: search-snippet (direct fetch blocked: expired TLS cert)

sources.title: SVF系列大功率船用变频岸电电源设备
sources.publisher: 江门市安利电源工程有限公司 (Jiangmen Anli)
sources.finding: SVF-series high-power marine shore-power frequency-converter supplies - second Chinese merchant vendor in the 250 kVA-MVA band.

sources.title: JJCY系列港口船用岸电电源
sources.finding: Third Chinese vendor with catalog port/marine shore-power supply series.

sources.title: 聚焦海环法 - 船舶岸电使用的难点在哪里？
sources.publisher: 信德海事 Xinde Marine News
sources.finding: Shore-power underuse driven by economics: two-part tariffs (energy + capacity charges); effective cost cited ~6.9 CNY/kWh Shanghai, ~3.56 CNY/kWh Shenzhen vs cheaper aux-engine fuel; retrofit payback long. Utilization is a price problem, not a converter-hardware problem.

sources.title: 价格机制或将扭转船舶岸电应用尴尬局面
sources.finding: Industry consensus that price mechanism, not equipment availability, gates shore-power usage.

sources.title: 打造上海国际航运中心岸电应用新高地 (提案)
sources.finding: Shanghai 2023: 18% connection-failure rate from voltage mismatch (real interop pain, steelman evidence); capacity-charge exemption for shore-power operators by end-2025; subsidy-dependence concerns - policy, not hardware, is the lever.

sources.title: FuelEU Maritime Q&A (Reg. EU 2023/1805)
sources.finding: OPS mandatory at berth from 1 Jan 2030 (AFIR Art.9 ports; 2035 all OPS-equipped ports) for CONTAINER and PASSENGER ships only; penalty = EUR 1.5 x established power demand x non-compliant hours. Vessel-side WTP is penalty-backed only for large 80005-class ships, not the compact tier.

sources.title: EU rules drive marine demand for shoreside power; infrastructure needed
sources.finding: EU shore-power demand driven by FuelEU/AFIR compliance calendar; buildout is port-tender EPC business.

sources.title: 关于示范推进国际航线集装箱船舶和邮轮靠港使用岸电行动方案(2023-2025年)
sources.publisher: 交通运输部 + 国家电网 + 南方电网
sources.finding: China shore-power push is a MOT + State Grid + CSG program - procurement flows through SOE/port-group channels where domestic vendors (Chuangtong et al.) hold standards authorship and installed base; de facto domestic-locked.
